title: Hunter's Chicken
subtitle: Chicken breast wrapped in streaky bacon with a smoky tomato sauce and melted Cheddar
excerpt: Hunter's chicken is a British pub kitchen staple: chicken breast wrapped in streaky bacon, baked in a smoky tomato sauce, and finished with melted Cheddar on top. It takes 40 minutes in the oven and produces exactly what you expect: a generous, satisfying plate.
sourceNotes: Contemporary British pub cooking. A staple of British chain restaurant menus from the 1990s onwards. Named loosely after the French 'chasseur' (hunter's) sauce tradition, though the British version is a distinct and simpler dish.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The tomato sauce can be made 2 days ahead and refrigerated. Assemble and bake on the day.

glossaryTerms.definition: A British pub kitchen dish of chicken breast wrapped in streaky bacon, baked in a barbecue-style tomato sauce, and topped with melted cheese. It became a standard of British chain restaurant menus in the 1990s and is now a fixture of home cooking. Despite the name, it has no direct connection to the French chasseur sauce tradition beyond a superficial resemblance.

A ground spice made from dried, smoked red peppers. Spanish pimentón de la Vera has the most pronounced smokiness. In tomato-based sauces, it gives a deep, charred sweetness that produces the characteristic barbecue note of hunter's chicken. One to two teaspoons is enough; more makes the sauce taste predominantly of paprika rather than tomato.

content.text: is straightforward. The only technique worth noting is wrapping: the bacon must overlap around the chicken breast tightly enough that it does not unravel in the oven. Secure each one with a cocktail stick if needed, removing before serving.

rows.symptom: Bacon falls away from the chicken during baking
rows.cause: Rashers not wrapped tightly enough, or chicken placed in sauce without seam-side down
rows.fix: Wrap tightly and place seam-side down in the dish. Use cocktail sticks to secure if needed.

rows.cause: Overcooked, or large breasts were not butterflied to an even thickness
rows.fix: Check the internal temperature at 25 minutes. Large breasts can be pounded to an even thickness before wrapping for more even cooking.

rows.cause: Sauce was not simmered long enough before the chicken was added
rows.fix: The sauce needs 15 minutes of simmering to cook out the tinned tomato sharpness and develop flavour. Do not skip this step.

rows.cause: Scattered too thickly in places, or oven too cool
rows.fix: Grate finely and scatter in an even layer. Return to the oven at a higher temperature (200°C) if the cheese is slow to melt.

content.text: Hunter's chicken is a British creation despite the name. It appeared on chain restaurant menus in the 1990s and became a staple of pub food culture: reliable, filling, and familiar. At home it is a useful weeknight dish because the sauce can be made in advance and the assembly is simple. Serve with chips, mashed potato, or a plain green salad.
